gaps: Part 3 tier table/pricing-logic mentions BYO sum-of-parts for Core (~$3,200) and Premium (~$8,400), but the Lite tier lacks an explicit BYO sum-of-parts comparison to demonstrate flagship-protection at the entry tier

gaps: Part 3 Pace upgrade pricing shows '–$0 (Deep, included free as quality signal)' which is confusing notation and contradicts Standard being $0 baseline
Memory preamble not addressed (acceptable if memory unavailable, but no signal given)
BYO Lite config check not shown — only Core and Premium flagship-protection math displayed
suggested_fixes: Clarify Pace pricing: make Standard the $0 baseline and either price Deep at $0 explicitly or remove the en-dash to avoid implying a discount
Silently omitting memory preamble is spec-compliant; no fix required unless memory was available
Add a one-line Lite flagship-protection check or note that Lite equals base by definition

gaps: Pricing-Anchor Rule violation: Input was 'Extremely vague' per test notes; spec requires placeholder tokens (+$A, +$B) for truly vague input, but output fabricates specific dollar amounts ($3,600, $12,000, $36,000, $600/session, etc.) by invoking 'domain-norm pricing' for generic 'coaching' — coaching is not a well-defined domain like exec coaching with clear norms until niche is specified
Preamble tone violation: Opens with 'You've given me almost nothing to work with' which edges toward punting/complaining; spec says infer and produce, not lampshade the vagueness
Pace dimension levels inconsistency in BYO: 'Monthly→Biweekly included in base' contradicts Lite config which is already Biweekly — base pricing logic is internally inconsistent
suggested_fixes: Replace all dollar figures with placeholder tokens (Lite=base $A, Core=$A+$B flagship, Premium=$A+$B+$C) since 'coaching' alone lacks tight enough norms to anchor — invoke domain norms only once user names exec/life/career/etc.
Drop the 'almost nothing to work with' opener; lead with a one-line assumption stub ('Assuming generic professional coaching; will diverge once you name niche') then produce
Reconcile Pace baseline: either set Lite to Monthly with Biweekly as a +$X upgrade, or remove the 'Monthly→Biweekly included' line

gaps: Part 3 tier table lacks an explicit per-tier 'Pricing logic / anchor' column or line — anchors are given for Starter/Growth/Pro as positioning lines but not labeled as pricing logic anchored to benchmarks or % of flagship

Flagship-protection check for Starter and Growth initially fails; skill requires flagships to remain the rational discount, and the response flags fixes but does not commit to a final locked spec, leaving the blueprint partially unresolved
Memory preamble not addressed (no statement that memory is unavailable is required, but SEO retainers are called out in spec as a well-known domain — anchors could have cited $ benchmarks explicitly)
suggested_fixes: Add a 'Pricing logic' line under each existing tier explicitly anchoring price (e.g., 'Starter: ~40% of flagship; entry diagnostic vs. $1.5–2.5K market floor')
Pick one concrete spec-addition path for Starter and Growth and lock it into the blueprint so flagship-protection math passes in the delivered output, not as a recommendation
Cite SEO retainer domain benchmarks ($ ranges) when anchoring each tier since the spec lists SEO retainers as a well-known-norms domain

gaps: Minor: Pricing-Anchor Rule — Studio pricing logic claims it 'sits clearly below your historical floor of $15K' but $18K is above the $15K floor, a small internal inconsistency.

Minor: Part 3 Signature flagship-protection math includes a quarterly hero motion averaged as ~$2.7K/mo but the per-dimension upgrade list prices Hero motion at +$8K each (one-time per piece), making the BYO sum derivation slightly opaque.

suggested_fixes: Reword Studio pricing logic to say it sits at/just above the historical floor rather than 'clearly below' $15K.
Clarify in BYO pricing whether Hero motion is one-time or recurring, and show the Signature BYO math step-by-step using consistent units.
Trim the follow-up menu to a single contextual recommendation per the spec.

gaps: Part 3 tier count: spec requires exactly three tiers + BYO; output presents Open Source (free) + Operator + Studio + Agency = four tiers, risking the 'five tiers instead of three' anti-pattern even though OSS is framed as 'the funnel'

Pricing-Anchor Rule application: Operator tier anchor cites '~$500–1,500' Maven course and '$300–500' consulting hour without clearly grounding these as domain norms vs. fabricated — borderline on the 'do not fabricate' rule

Memory preamble format: spec requires 'I've seen N similar offerings ([verticals]). Load-bearing dimensions historically: [list]' — output's preamble is freeform meta-commentary, not the prescribed schema
BYO floor reasoning: floor is stated as $290 but the rule 'anything cheaper routes to free OSS' implies the free tier is actually the floor, creating ambiguity with the three-tier constraint
suggested_fixes: Reframe Open Source as a pre-tier 'free funnel/floor' explicitly outside the three-tier count, or collapse it into the BYO floor to preserve the strict 3+BYO structure
Label the Operator anchor numbers as 'domain benchmarks (Maven cohort pricing, common consulting hourly)' or switch to placeholder tokens if not verifiable
Rewrite the memory preamble to match the exact spec template: 'Memory note: I've seen N similar offerings ([verticals]). Load-bearing dimensions historically: [list]...'
Clarify BYO floor: state that the paid floor is $290 and sub-floor demand auto-routes to the free OSS tier, explicitly distinguishing free distribution from the three paid tiers
